metadata.title: Cigarra Foundation — 501(c)(3) Nonprofit Transforming Lives in Bogotá, Colombia
metadata.description: The Cigarra Foundation is a U.S.-registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit serving 180+ children in Ciudad Bolívar, Bogotá with education, art, nutrition, and culture programs since 2002. All donations are tax-deductible.

hero.title: We transform lives through
hero.description: 1,877 children and youth in Ciudad Bolívar, Bogotá have found a path to a better future through our 13 programs. Your tax-deductible donation makes it possible.
hero.imageAlt: Children from the Cigarra Foundation in Bogotá, Colombia

programs.title: Our Programs
programs.subtitle: 13 education, art, and culture programs for the children of Ciudad Bolívar
programs.gridSubtitle: Each program is an open door to knowledge, creativity, and the comprehensive development of our beneficiaries.
programs.ctaTitle: Share your talent with those who need it most
programs.ctaDescription: Our programs need passionate people willing to share their knowledge. Your time and talent can open doors that change lives.
programs.supportProgram: Support this program with your donation
programs.defaultFullDescription: This program is part of the 13 programs offered by the Cigarra Foundation to children and youth in Ciudad Bolivar, Bogota. Through educational and recreational activities, we seek to contribute to the comprehensive development of participants.

descriptions.primera-infancia: Comprehensive care for children ages 0 to 5 with early stimulation, play, and family support.
descriptions.refuerzo-escolar: Academic support in math, reading, writing, and homework. We strengthen school learning.
descriptions.danza: Folk and contemporary dance. Body expression, coordination, and cultural appreciation.
descriptions.ingles: English language learning as a tool for accessing global opportunities.
descriptions.musica: Music education with string, wind, and percussion instruments. Artistic talent and discipline development.
descriptions.artes: Comprehensive artistic training that develops creativity, expression, and aesthetic sensitivity.
descriptions.escuela-de-artes: Artistic training space with specialized workshops in different creative disciplines.
descriptions.tecnologia: Training in computer tools and digital skills for the 21st century.
descriptions.biblioteca: A reading and book lending space to foster reading habits and access to written culture.
descriptions.psicologia: Psychosocial support for children, youth, and families, strengthening emotional well-being.
descriptions.grupo-mayores: Recreational, cultural, and wellness activities for senior members of the community.
descriptions.talleres-para-padres: Workshops for parents and caregivers on parenting, communication, and school support.
descriptions.ropero: Clothing and footwear donation and distribution for community families.

primera-infancia.longDescription: We provide comprehensive care for children ages 0 to 5 with early stimulation, play, art, and family support. The program focuses on cognitive, motor, social, and emotional development during the first years of life.

refuerzo-escolar.longDescription: We provide personalized academic support in math, Spanish, science, and other subjects. Our tutors help students improve their school performance, develop study habits, and strengthen basic skills.

danza.longDescription: Our dance program offers training in folk and contemporary dance. Participants develop body expression, coordination, rhythm, and a deep appreciation for Colombian culture through movement and music.

ingles.longDescription: The English program offers language learning as a tool for accessing global opportunities. Students develop comprehension, reading, writing, and conversation skills through dynamic and participatory methodologies.

musica.longDescription: Our music program offers comprehensive training in string, wind, and percussion instruments. Students develop musical skills, discipline, and teamwork through individual and group practice. We have a musical ensemble that performs at community events and festivals.

artes.longDescription: The Arts program offers comprehensive artistic training including visual arts, drawing, painting, and crafts. Participants develop their creativity, expression, and aesthetic sensitivity through individual and collective projects.

escuela-de-artes.longDescription: The School of Arts is an artistic training space that brings together different creative disciplines. Students participate in specialized workshops in visual arts, music, dance, and theater, strengthening their comprehensive artistic education.

tecnologia.longDescription: The technology program offers training in computer tools, basic programming, and digital skills for the 21st century. Students learn to use computers, safe internet practices, and office tools.

biblioteca.longDescription: Our community library is a space for reading, book lending, and reading promotion workshops. Beneficiaries can explore the world through reading, participate in book clubs, and activities that strengthen their reading comprehension skills.

psicologia.longDescription: Our psychology service offers psychosocial support for children, youth, and families. Through individual care, group workshops, and parent school, we strengthen emotional well-being and family relationships.

grupo-mayores.longDescription: We offer recreational, cultural, and wellness activities for senior members of the community. The program includes crafts, adapted physical activity, social gatherings, and support to improve their quality of life.

talleres-para-padres.longDescription: Parent Workshops offer meetings for parents and caregivers on positive parenting, effective communication, school support, and child development. We strengthen family bonds as the foundation of children's well-being.

ropero.longDescription: The community clothing closet receives, organizes, and distributes clothing and footwear donations for community families. This program ensures beneficiaries have access to clothing in good condition.

about.title: About Us
about.heroDescription: A U.S.-registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit with over two decades dedicated to transforming lives through art, education, and culture in one of Bogotá's most underserved communities.
about.missionStatement: To provide comprehensive care to children and youth in vulnerable conditions in Ciudad Bolivar, through education, art, culture, and recreation programs, promoting their personal and social development as agents of change in their community.
about.visionStatement: To be a leading organization in the social transformation of vulnerable communities in Colombia, recognized for the quality and impact of its educational, artistic, and cultural programs, and for the formation of citizens committed to social change.

timeline.title: Our Journey
timeline.subtitle: More than two decades of history building dreams and transforming realities in Ciudad Bolívar.

2002.title: Cigarra Foundation is Born
2002.item1: Dr. Carolyn Acosta Allen founds the Cigarra Foundation in response to the displacement crisis in Colombia.
2002.item2: An urgent need is identified in Puertas del Paraíso (Ciudad Bolívar): a safe place for children while their parents work.
2002.item3: With community support and Servivienda, construction begins in May; by August it was finished and in September it opens its doors.
2002.item4: Begins serving approximately 120 children (ages 3 months to 9 years).
2002.item5: Knitting courses for mothers are also launched.
2002.item6: The Foundation secures basic services (water, electricity, sewage) and, together with the community, builds two access staircases.

2002-2003.title: First Support and Social Expansion
2002-2003.item1: Vaccination drives and health brigades are carried out.
2002-2003.item2: For one year, Médecins Sans Frontières provides primary care.
2002-2003.item3: Dental services are offered with volunteers.
2002-2003.item4: The World Food Programme supports with food and supplements in the early years.

2003.title: First Major International Donation
2003.item1: In May 2003, the first major international donation arrives (anonymous benefactor), a support that continues over time.

2009.title: Children's Playground
2009.item1: The children's playground is built together with the community.

2010.title: Technology and Entrepreneurship Training
2010.item1: The "Cigarrita" computer room is remodeled and equipped (donation from IRT, United Kingdom).
2010.item2: A bakery space is rented, with baking and pastry courses (mediated by FUMDIR).

2011.title: Casa Na
2011.item2: The bakery and a nursing station are permanently installed there.
2011.item3: In the previously rented space, a community clothing closet opens (alternative income source).

2013.title: The Malala Room is Born
2013.item1: The Malala Room is created, a space for teenagers (12+ years) during alternate hours.

2014.title: Senior Citizens and Community Works
2014.item1: A senior citizens group is incorporated, strengthening intergenerational exchange.
2014.item2: With the community and Fundación Techo, the main access road to the Foundation is paved.

2015.title: New Dining Hall and Infrastructure
2015.item1: A new dining hall is inaugurated, plus two classrooms, bathrooms, and an administrative office.

2017-2019.title: New Building for Clothing Closet and Music
2017-2019.item1: A three-story building is constructed for the clothing closet and music rooms.
2017-2019.item2: The vision of promoting the music program and a future orchestra is strengthened.

2019.title: Greta Thunberg Building
2019.item1: Recycled containers are acquired and the Greta Thunberg Building (2 floors) is constructed.
2019.item2: The San Jorge Room and the gymnastics room operate there.

2020.title: Racamandaca Mascot
2020.item1: The Foundation adopts its mascot Racamandaca, inspired by "las cigarritas".

2022.title: House Competition and Musical Momentum
2022.item1: The house competition begins (Jaguar, Shark, Caiman, and Condor), inspired by the English model.
2022.item2: The orchestra dream is strengthened thanks to a donation from a major Colombian NGO.
2022.item3: The Foundation's anthem is recorded (December 2022, with Claraluna and music teachers).

2023.title: First Orchestra Concert
2023.item1: The first orchestra concert takes place (June 2023), an institutional milestone.

2025.title: 23 Years of Impact
2025.item1: The Foundation reports over 23 years of service.
2025.item2: It has benefited 1,090 families and 1,877 children and adolescents.
2025.item3: It is consolidated as a benchmark for comprehensive care, education, art, sports, technology, and psychosocial support.

2026.title: San Jorge Room Expansion
2026.item1: The expansion of the San Jorge Room capacity is projected, creating the San Jorgito Room for children ages 6 to 12.

footer.supportDescription: The Cigarra Foundation is a U.S.-registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Your donation is 100% tax-deductible and directly supports 180+ children in Bogotá, Colombia.
footer.description: We build real opportunities for more than 1,877 children and youth in Ciudad Bolívar, Bogotá.

taxDeductible.a: Yes! The Cigarra Foundation is a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization recognized by the IRS (EIN: 68-0505337). Your sponsorship payments are fully tax-deductible to the extent allowed by U.S. law. You'll receive an annual tax receipt for your records.

cancel.q: Can I cancel or change my plan at any time?
cancel.a: Absolutely. You can cancel or change your sponsorship plan at any time with no penalties. Simply contact us by email or WhatsApp, and we'll process the change immediately. If you cancel, the child will continue to be served through other resources.

graduation.q: What happens if my sponsored child graduates or leaves?
graduation.a: If your sponsored child graduates or leaves the foundation, we'll notify you and introduce you to a new child so you can continue your impact. You can also choose to redirect your contribution to another program.

payment.q: How do I pay from the United States?
payment.a: You can pay securely through PayPal using any major credit card, debit card, or your PayPal balance. All transactions are in U.S. dollars. You can set up automatic recurring payments for your convenience.

visit.q: Can I visit the foundation and meet my sponsored child?
visit.a: Yes! We welcome sponsors to visit us in Bogotá. We organize annual events where sponsors can meet their children, and you can also arrange a visit on weekdays by contacting us in advance. Many U.S.-based sponsors visit during their trips to Colombia.

percentage.q: What percentage of my donation goes directly to the children?
percentage.a: 100% of your donation goes directly to our programs. Administrative costs are covered by corporate partners. We publish annual audited financial reports and are fully transparent about how every dollar is spent.

howToHelp.volunteerWhatsAppDescription: Message us on WhatsApp and we'll tell you how to participate
howToHelp.faqSubtitle: We answer your questions so you can help with confidence.
howToHelp.finalCtaTitle: A child is waiting for you today
howToHelp.finalCtaDescription: Every day without action is one less opportunity in a child's life. Your 100% tax-deductible gift today builds a brighter tomorrow.

howDonate.q: How can I donate from the United States?
howDonate.a: You can donate securely online using PayPal or any major credit/debit card. All transactions are processed in U.S. dollars. You can make a one-time gift or set up a recurring monthly donation.

taxDeductible.a: Yes! The Cigarra Foundation is a registered 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization recognized by the IRS (EIN: 68-0505337). Your donation is fully tax-deductible to the extent allowed by U.S. law. You will receive a tax receipt for your records.

transparency.q: How do I know my donation is used correctly?
transparency.a: We publish annual financial reports and undergo independent audits. 100% of your donation goes directly to our programs — administrative costs are covered by corporate partners. Sponsors also receive personalized progress reports on their sponsored child.

canVolunteer.q: Can I volunteer from the U.S.?
canVolunteer.a: Yes! We welcome remote volunteers for translation, social media, fundraising, and mentoring. You can also visit us in Bogotá for in-person volunteering. Contact us via email or WhatsApp to learn about available opportunities.

whatIsPlanPadrino.q: What is the Child Sponsorship Plan?
whatIsPlanPadrino.a: The Sponsorship Plan allows you to sponsor a specific child, covering their education, daily meals, and access to all 13 of our programs. Starting at just $25/month, you'll receive personalized progress reports and photos throughout the year.

andrea.name: Andrea Morales
andrea.quote: Volunteering at the Cigarra Foundation has been the most enriching experience of my life. Watching children advance academically and gain confidence in themselves is priceless.

carlos.name: Carlos Gutiérrez
carlos.quote: I share my love for music with the children of Ciudad Bolívar every Saturday. The joy on their faces when they manage to play a complete song is the best reward I could receive.

laura.name: Laura Jiménez
laura.quote: From the communications team, I help more people learn about the foundation's work. Every story we tell inspires someone new to join this cause.

padrinoSection.taxDescription: As a 501(c)(3) organization, donations to the Cigarra Foundation are fully deductible on your U.S. federal tax return under IRS Section 170.
padrinoSection.taxBenefit1: Full tax deduction on the donated amount applied to your federal income tax return (IRS Section 170).
padrinoSection.taxBenefit2: Tax receipt: The Cigarra Foundation provides an official donation receipt for your tax filing, delivered promptly by email.
padrinoSection.taxBenefit3: Corporate donors may also qualify for state-level tax benefits depending on your state of incorporation.
padrinoSection.includesTitle: What does the Sponsorship Plan include?

corporate.esalDescription: The Cigarra Foundation is a U.S.-registered 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization recognized by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). This means all donations from U.S. individuals and corporations are fully tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law. We are also registered as a nonprofit (ESAL) in Colombia.
corporate.esalLegalDescription: As a 501(c)(3) organization, the Cigarra Foundation meets all IRS requirements for tax-exempt status. Donors can deduct their contributions on their federal tax returns under IRS Section 170. We provide official tax receipts for all donations. Our EIN (Employer Identification Number) is 68-0505337.
corporate.impactSubtitle: Over two decades of transparent and measurable work. These are the numbers that back our mission.
corporate.partnersSubtitle: We have the support of companies and organizations committed to the social development of Ciudad Bolívar. Our corporate partners are a fundamental part of the impact we generate.
corporate.transformingDescription: Over more than 23 years, we have built strong partnerships with private sector companies, international organizations, and government institutions. Each partner contributes uniquely to the well-being of our beneficiaries.

alliancesList.nationalInternational: Partnerships with national and international organizations

esalLegal.compliance: Full compliance with U.S. and Colombian nonprofit regulations

home.title: Cigarra Foundation — 501(c)(3) Nonprofit | Donate to Help Children in Colombia
home.description: The Cigarra Foundation is an IRS-recognized 501(c)(3) nonprofit serving 180+ children in Bogotá, Colombia since 2002. Tax-deductible donations fund education, nutrition, and arts programs.

about.title: About the Cigarra Foundation — 23 Years Transforming Lives in Colombia
about.description: Since 2002, the Cigarra Foundation has served 1,877 children and 1,090 families in Ciudad Bolívar, Bogotá through 13 education, art, and culture programs. 501(c)(3) nonprofit.

programs.title: Our 13 Programs — Education, Arts & Nutrition for Children in Colombia
programs.description: 13 free programs for children in Bogotá: academic tutoring, music, dance, arts, technology, library, psychology, and nutrition. Funded by tax-deductible donations.

howToHelp.title: Donate to Children in Colombia — Tax-Deductible 501(c)(3) | Cigarra Foundation
howToHelp.description: Make a tax-deductible donation via PayPal. Sponsor a child from $25/month. 100% of your gift funds education and meals for children in Bogotá. 501(c)(3) EIN: 68-0505337.

news.title: News & Updates — Cigarra Foundation
news.description: The latest news, events, and achievements from the Cigarra Foundation in Ciudad Bolívar, Bogotá, Colombia.

successStories.title: Success Stories — Lives Transformed by the Cigarra Foundation
successStories.description: Read real stories of children whose lives have been transformed through education, art, and community support at the Cigarra Foundation.

contact.title: Contact Us — Cigarra Foundation
contact.description: Get in touch with the Cigarra Foundation. We're based in Ciudad Bolívar, Bogotá, Colombia and serve donors worldwide.

anthem.title: Foundation Anthem — Cigarra Foundation
anthem.description: Listen to the official Cigarra Foundation anthem, a symbol of hope and transformation performed by our children and youth.

team.title: Our Team — 33+ Professionals Making a Difference
team.description: Meet the educators, artists, psychologists, and administrators behind the Cigarra Foundation's 13 programs serving children in Bogotá.

planPadrino.title: Sponsor a Child in Colombia — From $25/Month | Tax-Deductible 501(c)(3)
planPadrino.description: Sponsor a child's education and daily meals from $25/month. Receive progress reports and photos. 100% tax-deductible — Cigarra Foundation is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit.

volunteer.title: Volunteer With Us — Cigarra Foundation
volunteer.description: Volunteer remotely or in person with the Cigarra Foundation. Share your skills in education, arts, technology, or communications to help children in Colombia.

corporateImpact.title: Corporate Partnerships — Cigarra Foundation 501(c)(3)
corporateImpact.description: Partner with a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Corporate donations are tax-deductible. Sponsor programs, organize volunteer days, or fund education for children in Colombia.

notFound.title: Page Not Found
notFound.message: Sorry, the page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.

anthem.title: Anthem of the Cigarra Foundation
anthem.subtitle: Our song of hope and transformation
anthem.watchVideoSubtitle: Watch and listen to the official anthem of the Cigarra Foundation performed by our children and youth.
anthem.attribution: Official anthem of the Cigarra Foundation — Ciudad Bolívar, Bogotá
anthem.ctaDescription: The anthem reflects the spirit of transformation that our children and youth experience every day through our 13 programs.

donationCheckout.title: Make a tax-deductible gift
donationCheckout.subtitle: 100% of your donation feeds, educates, and protects a child in Ciudad Bolívar. We are a 501(c)(3) nonprofit.
donationCheckout.errorCreating: There was an error processing your donation. Please try again or contact us.
donationCheckout.impact30000: Nutritious snacks for 4 children for a week
donationCheckout.impact50000: School supplies for 1 child for 1 month
donationCheckout.impact100000: Nutritious meals for 1 child for a full month
donationCheckout.impact200000: Complete education + meals program for 1 child
donationCheckout.impact500000: Full educational scholarship for 1 child for a semester
donationCheckout.impactCustom: Every dollar counts to transform a life
donationCheckout.impact5: a day of nutritious snacks for a child
donationCheckout.impact10: a week of school supplies for a child
donationCheckout.impact25: a week of nutritious meals for a child
donationCheckout.impact50: a full week of education + meals for a child
donationCheckout.impact100: a complete month of education + meals for a child
donationCheckout.impactCustomUSD: Every dollar you give transforms a child's life
donationCheckout.paypalCancelled: Payment was cancelled. You can try again whenever you're ready.
donationCheckout.taxReceipt: You will receive a tax receipt by email for your records.

donationResponse.pageDescription: Check the status of your donation to Fundación Cigarra.
donationResponse.approvedMessage: Your donation of {amount} has been received successfully. You will receive a confirmation email.
donationResponse.rejectedMessage: Your payment could not be processed. Please try a different payment method.
donationResponse.pendingMessage: Your payment is being verified. We will notify you by email when confirmed.
donationResponse.failedMessage: There was an error processing your payment. Please try again.

donateLanding.title: Your gift changes
donateLanding.subtitle: 100% of your tax-deductible donation goes directly to education, daily meals, and arts programs for 180+ children in Bogotá, Colombia.

education.title: Education & Academic Tutoring
education.description: Homework help, exam prep, and personalized academic support so children stay in school and thrive.

nutrition.title: Daily Nutritious Meals
nutrition.description: Breakfast, lunch, and afternoon snacks for just $1.80/day per child — fueling growing minds and bodies.

arts.title: Music, Dance & Arts Programs
arts.description: String, wind, and percussion instruments, folk dance, visual arts — creativity as a vehicle for healing and growth.

psychology.title: Psychology & Family Support
psychology.description: Psychosocial support for children and families, strengthening emotional well-being and family bonds.

donateLanding.photoCaption: Children learning music at the Cigarra Foundation — Ciudad Bolívar, Bogotá
donateLanding.legalDescription: The Cigarra Foundation (EIN: 68-0505337) is a tax-exempt organization under IRS Section 501(c)(3). Your donation is fully tax-deductible to the extent allowed by U.S. law. You will receive a tax receipt by email.
